note: Verifies embedded-library behaviors of LanceDB 0.34.0 over local files — no server, no Docker, no GPU, no network. EXP B/D are bit-deterministic. EXP E is an approximate-index recall measurement (seeded). EXP F is BM25 ranking whose scores are tantivy-build-dependent. Values below are from the firsthand notes at authoring (2026-07-09); the `reproduction_2026_07_18` block records a re-run of the reconstructed harness on the same pinned 0.34.0.
subject: lancedb 0.34.0 (Python), pyarrow 24.0.0

behaviors_verified.claim: lancedb.connect(dir) returns an in-process handle (LanceDBConnection) — no daemon, data lives as on-disk .lance files.
behaviors_verified.evidence: probe-result.json.connection_type == 'LanceDBConnection'; firsthand-benchmark EXP A/C on-disk layout

behaviors_verified.claim: Exact top-k over fixed 2D vectors is deterministic and `_distance` is *squared* L2 (0.02 = 0.01+0.01, not 0.14) — a common gotcha.
Every write bumps a version; you can checkout an old version (4 rows) and return to latest (5 rows).
IVF_PQ approximate index trades recall for speed; default nprobes gives recall@10 ~0.7, and nprobes/refine_factor tuning recovers it toward 1.0.
Full-text (BM25) search works with zero embeddings via create_index(config=FTS()); create_fts_index was deprecated in 0.25.0.

reproduction_2026_07_18.harness: reconstructed probe.py, re-run on the same pinned lancedb==0.34.0
reproduction_2026_07_18.exp_d: EXACT match (v1=4 rows, latest=5 rows, 2 versions)
reproduction_2026_07_18.exp_e: recall_default 0.7 EXACT; tuned nprobes64/refine10 = 0.9 on re-run vs 1.0 in the authoring note. PQ codebook (k-means) init is not fully pinned, so the tuned recall lands in the 0.9–1.0 band — the *direction* (tuning recovers most of the lost recall) reproduces, the exact last decimal does not.
reproduction_2026_07_18.exp_f: fox top-2 ids [3,0] match; BM25 scores drift (0.7549/0.6407 vs 0.7199/0.6236) and 'lazy' returns [1] not [1,0]. The FTS corpus text was NOT recorded in the 2026-07-09 notes, so probe.py reconstructs a plausible corpus — ids for 'fox' reproduce, absolute BM25 scores are tantivy-build- and corpus-dependent and are not claimed as bit-exact.

cited_not_measured.claim: Latest stable at authoring: Python 0.34.0 / Node·Rust 0.31.0.
Official positioning shifted from 'vector database' to 'OSS embedded retrieval library for multimodal AI' / 'multimodal lakehouse'.
Lance is an open columnar lakehouse format; LanceDB is the embedded retrieval library on top. OSS (local embedded) and Cloud/Enterprise (managed, petabyte-scale) share the table format.
